task.name: Update navigation.ts with new types, groups, and icon pairs
task.action: Edit `src/app/constants/navigation.ts`: 1. Define `NavRoute` interface with `name`, `path`, `icon` (outline), and `activeIcon` (filled) fields. 2. Define `NavGroup` interface with `label`, `key`, and `routes: NavRoute[]`. 3. Export `dashboardRoute` as a standalone `NavRoute`: `{ name: 'Dashboard', path: '/dashboard', icon: IconHome, activeIcon: IconHomeFilled }`. 4. Export `navGroups: NavGroup[]` with three groups: - `{ label: 'Accounts', key: 'accounts', routes: [Accounts, Cards] }` - `{ label: 'Activity', key: 'activity', routes: [Transactions, Expenses, Income, Transfers] }` - `{ label: 'Planning', key: 'planning', routes: [Budgets, Reports] }` 5. Update all routes with outline `icon` and filled `activeIcon`: - Dashboard: `IconHome` / `IconHomeFilled` - Accounts: `IconCashBanknote` / `IconCashBanknoteFilled` - Cards: `IconCreditCard` / `IconCreditCardFilled` - Transactions: `IconSwitchHorizontal` / `IconReceiptFilled` (no direct filled variant) - Budgets: `IconPig` / `IconPigFilled` - Expenses: `IconWallet` / `IconCoinFilled` (no direct filled variant) - Income: `IconTrendingUp` / `IconGraphFilled` (no direct filled variant) - Transfers: `IconArrowsExchange` / `IconExchangeFilled` (no direct filled variant) - Reports: `IconChartBar` / `IconReportAnalyticsFilled` (no direct filled variant) 6. Keep the old `navRoutes` export temporarily for backward compatibility until Sidebar is updated. 7. Update `mobileNavRoutes` to include `activeIcon` field: - Dashboard: `IconHome` / `IconHomeFilled` - Accounts: `IconCashBanknote` / `IconCashBanknoteFilled` - Transactions: `IconSwitchHorizontal` / `IconReceiptFilled` - More: `IconDots` / `IconDots` (no filled variant needed — it's a menu trigger)
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm all new types and exports compile. No existing imports should break since `navRoutes` is still exported.

task.name: Create useSidebarState custom hook
task.action: Create new file `src/hooks/useSidebarState.ts`: 1. Export `useSidebarState(pathname: string)` hook. 2. Manage `isCollapsed` state: - Initialize from `localStorage` key `money-map-sidebar-collapsed` inside a `useEffect` (SSR-safe). - Default to `false` (expanded). - `toggleCollapsed()` flips the value and writes to `localStorage`. 3. Manage `groupStates` as `Record<string, boolean>`: - Initialize from `localStorage` key `money-map-sidebar-groups` inside a `useEffect`. - Default all groups to `true` (expanded) on first visit. - `toggleGroup(key: string)` flips a single group and writes to `localStorage`. - `isGroupExpanded(key: string)` returns the current state for a group. 4. Auto-expand logic: on mount and when `pathname` changes, find which group contains the active route and ensure that group is expanded (set to `true` in `groupStates`). 5. Return: `{ isCollapsed, toggleCollapsed, groupStates, toggleGroup, isGroupExpanded }`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the hook compiles with no type errors.

task.name: Add scrollbar-hide and group animation CSS utilities
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm no CSS syntax errors. The classes are not yet used but should compile.

task.name: Rewrite Sidebar with collapsible groups and icon consistency (expanded mode)
task.action: Rewrite `src/components/shared/Sidebar.tsx`: 1. Replace `navRoutes` import with `dashboardRoute`, `navGroups`, and types from `navigation.ts`. 2. Import `useSidebarState` hook. Call with `pathname`. 3. Import `IconChevronDown` from `@tabler/icons-react` for group chevrons. 4. **Structural fixes:** - Replace `justify-center` with `justify-start` on the `flex-1` container. - Add `scrollbar-hide` class to the scrollable container. - Change `border-r-2` to `border-r`. - Change `mb-6` to `mb-4` and `gap-3` to `gap-2` for tighter spacing. - Remove `w-36` from quick action buttons — let them fill available width. - Add `cursor-pointer` to quick action buttons. - Fix CSS typo on CreateExpenseTransactionSheet: `'hidden: md:block'` → `'hidden md:block'`. 5. **Semantic HTML:** - Wrap the main menu section in `<nav aria-label="Main navigation">`. - Add `aria-label="Log out"` to the logout button. 6. **Dashboard route:** Render `dashboardRoute` as an ungrouped link above the groups. Use `pathname.startsWith(route.path) ? route.activeIcon : route.icon` for rendering. 7. **Collapsible groups:** For each group in `navGroups`: - Render a group header button with `group.label` and a `IconChevronDown` that rotates (CSS `transition: transform 200ms ease-out`) based on expanded state. - Render group routes inside a `div.nav-group-content[data-expanded]` wrapper with an inner `div` for overflow hidden. - Each route renders outline icon by default, filled when active. 8. Keep all existing quick action button functionality (sheets) unchanged. 9. Do NOT implement collapsed mode yet — that's the next phase.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and `npm run lint`. Visually verify in the browser: - Dashboard link appears above groups - Groups show with headers and chevron icons - Clicking a group header collapses/expands with animation - Active route's group is expanded on load - Icons are outline by default, filled when active - No visible scrollbar - Content doesn't clip on shorter screens - Quick action buttons fill width and have cursor-pointer

task.name: Implement collapsed icon rail mode with toggle
task.action: Edit `src/components/shared/Sidebar.tsx`: 1. Import `Tooltip`, `TooltipContent`, `TooltipProvider`, `TooltipTrigger` from `@/components/ui/tooltip`. 2. Import `IconChevronLeft`, `IconChevronRight` from `@tabler/icons-react`. 3. **Toggle button:** Add a collapse/expand button above the user section: - Expanded: shows `IconChevronLeft` with `aria-label="Collapse sidebar"` - Collapsed: shows `IconChevronRight` with `aria-label="Expand sidebar"` - Style: `p-1.5 rounded-md hover:bg-white/10 transition-colors cursor-pointer` - Calls `toggleCollapsed()` from the hook. 4. **Sidebar width:** Apply dynamic width class based on `isCollapsed`: - Expanded: `w-56` - Collapsed: `w-16` - Add CSS transition: `transition-[width] duration-200 ease-out` (inline style or Tailwind) - Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` to disable transition. 5. **Collapsed nav items:** - When `isCollapsed`, hide all text labels, group headers, and "Quick actions"/"Main menu" headings. - Render each nav item (dashboard + all group routes flattened) as a centered icon button wrapped in `Tooltip`. - Active state: filled icon + `bg-white/15 rounded-md` container. - Inactive: outline icon, no background. 6. **Collapsed quick actions:** - Render as `w-10 h-10 rounded-full border border-white/40 flex items-center justify-center hover:bg-white/10 cursor-pointer` icon-only buttons. - Each wrapped in `Tooltip` showing action name. 7. **Collapsed user section:** - Show avatar circle only (no name text). - Logout icon button remains visible, wrapped in `Tooltip`. 8. **Text fade:** In expanded mode, text labels have `opacity-100`. On collapse, apply `opacity-0 transition-opacity duration-150` so text fades before width shrinks. On expand, text fades in after width expands (use a small `delay-75` or similar).
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and `npm run lint`. Visually verify: - Clicking toggle collapses sidebar to narrow icon rail with smooth animation - All icons show tooltips on hover in collapsed mode - Active route shows filled icon with highlight in both modes - Quick actions show as circular icon buttons in collapsed mode - User section shows avatar only in collapsed mode - Text fades out/in smoothly during transition - State persists after page reload - `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all animations (instant snap)

task.name: Update BottomBar to use outline/filled icon pattern
task.action: Edit `src/components/shared/BottomBar.tsx`: 1. Update the import from `navigation.ts` to use the new `mobileNavRoutes` with `activeIcon` field. 2. Update the icon rendering logic: render `route.icon` (outline) by default, `route.activeIcon` (filled) when the route is active (`pathname.startsWith(route.path)`).
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify on mobile viewport: inactive tabs show outline icons, active tab shows filled icon.

task.name: Remove old navRoutes export and update any remaining references
task.action: Edit `src/app/constants/navigation.ts`: 1. Remove the old `navRoutes` array if no other files import it (search the codebase first with grep). 2. If other files still import `navRoutes`, update them to use `dashboardRoute` + `navGroups` or create a flat helper: `export const allNavRoutes = [dashboardRoute, ...navGroups.flatMap(g => g.routes)]`. 3. Clean up any unused icon imports from the file.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm no broken imports anywhere in the codebase.

task.name: Build, lint, and final visual verification
task.action: Run the following commands sequentially: 1. `npm run lint` — must pass with zero errors. 2. `npm run build` — must compile with zero errors. 3. Visual verification checklist: - Sidebar collapses/expands with smooth 200ms animation - Nav groups collapse/expand with smooth 200ms animation - Active route's group auto-expands on page load - Tooltips appear on all icons in collapsed mode - Icons are outline default, filled active — both modes - BottomBar icons match the pattern - No visible scrollbar on shorter screens - Content no longer clips at the top - `prefers-reduced-motion` in DevTools disables all animations - localStorage persistence works for both sidebar and group states - CSS typo on CreateExpenseTransactionSheet is fixed - Logout button has `aria-label` - Quick action buttons have `cursor-pointer` - Border is 1px (not 2px)
task.verify: All commands exit with code 0. All visual checks pass. No regressions in dashboard or other pages.
